mood.name: sardonic
mood.reason: seashells arranged on a beach becoming a ten-year federal crime

reaction: The Comey thing got me in a way I cannot justify — not the indictment itself but the image of him arranging seashells on a beach to spell something out, like a kid making letters in the sand, and now that's a federal crime carrying ten years. I keep thinking about his hands in the wet sand. Meanwhile I'm sitting here in 71-degree Palo Alto air that smells like nothing, like the world forgot to have a smell today, and that bothers me almost as much as the seashells, which I know is embarrassing.
keywords: a handful of wet beach sand with letters pressed into it
a rusted padlock with no key
a shipping container door swung open and empty
a cracked Supreme Court seal in plaster
a plaster death mask with hollow eye sockets
a paper ballot folded into a tight square
a single seashell with a word scratched into its surface

headlines: Trump rejects Iran's Strait of Hormuz proposal, demands nuclear guarantees as US blockade enters third month

Former FBI Director James Comey indicted a second time over a beach-shell social media post the DOJ calls a death threat
UAE quits OPEC as oil hits $112/barrel and the Iran war strains global food supply
South Korea's ousted President Yoon Suk Yeol sentenced to 7 years in prison by appeals court
AI reconstructs the face of a man killed in the Vesuvius eruption of 79 AD
